name: Garlic Parmesan Twists
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Gold Medal Better for Bread Flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Measure carefully. Place all ingredients except butter (or margarine), garlic, and Parmesan cheese in the bread pan in the order recommended by the manufacturer.
2. Select the Dough/Manual cycle.
3. At the end of the dough cycle, transfer the dough to a large greased bowl. Turn the bowl so the greased side is up. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 30 minutes, or until doubled in bulk. The dough is ready if an indentation remains when touched with the tip of a finger.
4. Grease a cookie sheet. Punch down the risen dough. Divide the dough into 20 equal pieces. Roll each piece into 10"-12-inch ropes on a lightly floured surface.
5. Pinch two ropes together at one end and twist, starting at the pinched end. Tuck the other end under and place the twist on the prepared baking sheet. Repeat with remaining ropes.
6. Mix butter (or margarine) with garlic and Parmesan; brush over twists. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 20-30 minutes, or until doubled.
7. Preheat oven to 400°F. Bake the twists for 12-15 minutes, or until golden brown. Serve warm, or cool on a wire rack.
